title: Eicosanoids وعوامل الالتهاب الدهنية
summary: الإيكوسانويدات مشتقات حمض الأراكيدونيك تشمل البروستاجلاندين والثرموبوكسان واللوكوترينات. تتشكل عبر مسارين: Cyclooxygenase (PG, TXA2) و5-Lipoxygenase (Leukotrienes). PGI2 يُوسع الأوعية ويثبط تجمع الصفائح بينما TXA2 يُضيق الأوعية ويُحرض تجمعها. اللوكوترينات (LTC4, LTD4) تُسبب تضيق الشعب في الربو. الكينين (Bradykinin) وسيط التهابي يسبب الألم والتوسع الوعائي.

key_drugs.name: Misoprostol
key_drugs.note: حماية المعدة عند تناول NSAIDs، إنهاء الحمل في الثلث الأول

key_drugs.name: Dinoprostone (PGE2)
key_drugs.note: تحريض الطلق وإنهاء الحمل في الثلث الثاني

key_drugs.name: Carboprost (PGF2α)
key_drugs.note: إجهاض الثلث الثاني، نزيف ما بعد الولادة

key_drugs.name: Alprostadil (PGE1)
key_drugs.note: علاج ضعف الانتصاب، الحفاظ على انفتاح القناة الشريانية

mnemonics.title: PGI2 vs TXA2: التوازن الوعائي
mnemonics.body:
PGI2 = 'باي باي للجلطة': Vasodilation + يثبط تجمع الصفائح (من جدار الأوعية)
TXA2 = 'تاكس على الأوعية': Vasoconstriction + يُحرض تجمع الصفائح (من الصفائح نفسها)
التوازن بينهما يحكم تكوين الجلطة

mnemonics.title: مسارا حمض الأراكيدونيك: COX vs LOX
mnemonics.body:
COX (Cyclooxygenase) = كوكس الطباخ:
- ينتج PG, TXA2
- يثبطه NSAIDs والكورتيزون
LOX (5-Lipoxygenase) = لوكس الضوء:
- ينتج Leukotrienes
- يثبطه Zileuton فقط
الكورتيزون يثبط كليهما (يثبط Phospholipase A2)

mnemonics.title: Misoprostol: الحارس والهادم
mnemonics.body: Misoprostol = 'ميسو يحمي ويهدم': يحمي معدة مريض NSAIDs (يقلل الحمض ويزيد المخاط)، وفي نفس الوقت يهدم الحمل في الثلث الأول مع Methotrexate. دواء واحد بدورين متضادين!

mnemonics.title: البروستاجلاندين: كل واحد له دور
mnemonics.body:
PGE = 'E للتوسع': يُوسع الأوعية ويُرخي العضلات الملساء (عدا الرحم بجرعة منخفضة يُقلصه)
PGF = 'F للانقباض': يُقلص الرحم والأوعية (يُستخدم للإجهاض)
PGD = 'D للرئة': تضيق الشعب الهوائية
PGI2 = 'I للإيقاف': يوقف الجلطة وتضيق الأوعية

mnemonics.title: Bradykinin وACEI: علاقة السبب والنتيجة
mnemonics.body: ACE = إنزيم يكسر Bradykinin. عندما نُعطي ACE Inhibitor نوقف كسر Bradykinin فيتراكم. Bradykinin المتراكم يسبب: سعال جاف (الأثر الجانبي الشهير لـ ACEIs) + وذمة وعائية. لذا ACEIs تُسبب سعالًا جافًا.

flashcards.front: What does the word Eicosanoids mean?
flashcards.back: Eicosa = 20 in Greek; they are 20-carbon fatty acid derivatives of arachidonic acid

flashcards.front: What are the two main pathways for converting arachidonic acid?
flashcards.back: Cyclooxygenase (COX) → PG + TXA2, and 5-Lipoxygenase (LOX) → Leukotrienes

flashcards.front: Which enzyme do NSAIDs inhibit?
flashcards.back: Cyclooxygenase (COX-1 and COX-2)

flashcards.front: Which drug inhibits Phospholipase A2 and blocks both pathways?
flashcards.back: Corticosteroids

flashcards.front: What is the effect of PGI2 on platelets?
flashcards.back: Inhibits platelet aggregation (anticoagulant)

flashcards.front: What is the effect of TXA2 on platelets?
flashcards.back: Induces platelet aggregation (procoagulant)

flashcards.front: What is the difference between PGE and PGF in the uterus?
flashcards.back: PGF contracts the uterus more strongly; PGE contracts it at low doses and relaxes it at high doses

flashcards.front: What is the use of Misoprostol with NSAIDs?
flashcards.back: Protects the gastric mucosa from the effects of NSAIDs (reduces acid and increases mucus)

flashcards.front: Which drug is used to treat primary pulmonary hypertension?
flashcards.back: Epoprostenol (PGI2) because it dilates the pulmonary vessels

flashcards.front: Which leukotrienes cause asthma?
flashcards.back: Cysteinyl leukotrienes: LTC4, LTD4, LTE4 (called SRS-A)

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Montelukast?
flashcards.back: Blocks leukotriene receptors (CysLT1), preventing bronchoconstriction

flashcards.front: What is Bradykinin and how is it formed?
flashcards.back: A vasodilator peptide formed from Kininogen by Kallikrein; degraded by ACE

flashcards.front: Why do ACE Inhibitors cause a dry cough?
flashcards.back: Because ACE normally degrades Bradykinin; when inhibited, Bradykinin accumulates and irritates cough receptors

flashcards.front: What is the use of Latanoprost?
flashcards.back: Eye drops for treating glaucoma (a PGF2α analog); increases aqueous humor outflow

flashcards.front: What is the role of leukotriene LTB4?
flashcards.back: Attracts neutrophils and induces inflammation

flashcards.front: What is the role of Aprotinin?
flashcards.back: A Kallikrein inhibitor that reduces Bradykinin formation; reduces bleeding in major surgery

flashcards.front: What is the role of prostaglandins in obstetrics?
flashcards.back: Induce uterine contractions; used for labor induction and termination of pregnancy

flashcards.front: What is the role of PGE2 in the fetal ductus arteriosus?
flashcards.back: Maintains its patency (Patent Ductus Arteriosus)

flashcards.front: What is the source of eicosanoids?
flashcards.back: Arachidonic acid from membrane phospholipids via the COX pathway (prostaglandins/thromboxane) or the LOX pathway (leukotrienes).

flashcards.front: What is the function of Thromboxane A2?
flashcards.back: Vasoconstriction and platelet aggregation (from platelets via COX-1).

flashcards.front: What is the function of Prostacyclin (PGI2)?
flashcards.back: Vasodilation and inhibition of platelet aggregation (from vascular endothelium).

flashcards.front: What is the use of Misoprostol?
flashcards.back: A PGE1 analog that protects the stomach from NSAID-induced ulcers and is used in obstetrics.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Montelukast/Zafirlukast?
flashcards.back: Leukotriene receptor antagonists - asthma prophylaxis.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Zileuton?
flashcards.back: 5-lipoxygenase enzyme inhibitor → reduces leukotriene synthesis.

flashcards.front: What are the kinins (Bradykinin)?
flashcards.back: Local vasodilator peptides that cause pain and increased permeability; degraded by ACE (kininase II).

mcqs.q: A patient chronically takes NSAIDs for joint pain. Which drug is given to protect his stomach?
mcqs.explanation: Misoprostol (synthetic PGE1) reduces acid secretion and increases protective gastric mucus, making it ideal with NSAIDs.

mcqs.q: Which drug is used to treat erectile dysfunction through local vasodilation?
mcqs.explanation: Alprostadil (PGE1) dilates the blood vessels in the corpus cavernosum and relaxes smooth muscle.

options: Both the COX and LOX pathways
mcqs.explanation: Corticosteroids inhibit Phospholipase A2, which produces arachidonic acid essential for both pathways.

mcqs.q: An asthmatic patient needs a drug that inhibits bronchoconstriction caused by LTC4 and LTD4. Which drug?
mcqs.explanation: Montelukast blocks CysLT1 receptors and prevents the effect of LTC4 and LTD4 on the airways.

mcqs.q: What is the effect of TXA2 on blood vessels and platelets?
options: Vasoconstriction and induction of platelet aggregation
mcqs.explanation: TXA2 is produced by platelets and causes vasoconstriction and induction of platelet aggregation (procoagulant).

mcqs.q: Why does an ACE Inhibitor cause a dry cough?
options: Because it inhibits Bradykinin breakdown, so it accumulates and irritates cough receptors
mcqs.explanation: ACE (Kininase II) normally degrades Bradykinin. ACEIs block this, so Bradykinin accumulates and causes the dry cough.

mcqs.q: Which drug is used to maintain patency of the ductus arteriosus in premature infants?
mcqs.explanation: Alprostadil (PGE1) keeps the ductus arteriosus open in premature infants who need it.

mcqs.q: Which leukotriene attracts neutrophils and induces inflammation?
mcqs.explanation: LTB4 is the main chemotactic agent for neutrophils and induces the inflammatory response.

mcqs.q: What is the difference between PGF2α and PGE2 in their effect on the uterus?
options: PGF2α contracts the uterus; PGE2 contracts it at low doses and relaxes it at high doses
mcqs.explanation: PGF2α is a strong uterine contractor. PGE2 has a dual effect depending on dose and receptor.

mcqs.q: What is the mechanism of action of Zileuton?
options: Inhibits the 5-Lipoxygenase enzyme, preventing leukotriene formation
mcqs.explanation: Zileuton is a 5-LOX enzyme inhibitor; it prevents conversion of arachidonic acid into leukotrienes.
TXA2 is a vasoconstrictor and platelet aggregator.

mcqs.q: Misoprostol protects the stomach because it is:
mcqs.explanation: A prostaglandin analog that enhances mucosal protection.
A leukotriene receptor blocker for asthma prophylaxis.
